title: Aspidium calcareum
taxonomy: Aspidium calcareum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of fern that is native to certain regions. Aspidium calcareum is a rare species and its classification can vary depending on the source. However, most sources agree on its placement within the plant kingdom. Aspidium calcareum is often found in specific habitats with high calcium content.
uses: Aspidium calcareum, also known as the calcareous spleenwort, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. It has been employed to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional Asian medicine, Aspidium calcareum is sometimes used to treat respiratory problems and gastrointestinal issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications in modern medicine.

intro: Aspidium calcareum, commonly known as the calcareous spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the Aspidium genus. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against cell damage.
